Package [org.apache.flink.api.connector.source](<package-summary.html>)

# Interface SourceReader<T,SplitT extends [SourceSplit](<SourceSplit.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")>

Type Parameters:
    `T` \- The type of the record emitted by this source reader.
    `SplitT` \- The type of the source splits.

All Superinterfaces:
    `[AutoCloseable](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/AutoCloseable.html> "class or interface in java.lang")`, `[CheckpointListener](<../../common/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.state")`

All Known Subinterfaces:
    `[ExternallyInducedSourceReader](<ExternallyInducedSourceReader.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")<T,SplitT>`

All Known Implementing Classes:
    `[DoubleEmittingSourceReaderWithCheckpointsInBetween](<../../../connector/datagen/source/DoubleEmittingSourceReaderWithCheckpointsInBetween.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.datagen.source")`, `[FileSourceReader](<../../../connector/file/src/impl/FileSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.file.src.impl")`, `[FromElementsSourceReader](<../../../connector/testframe/source/FromElementsSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.testframe.source")`, `[GeneratingIteratorSourceReader](<../../../connector/datagen/source/GeneratingIteratorSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.datagen.source")`, `[HybridSourceReader](<../../../connector/base/source/hybrid/HybridSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.hybrid")`, `[IteratorSourceReader](<lib/util/IteratorSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source.lib.util")`, `[IteratorSourceReaderBase](<lib/util/IteratorSourceReaderBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source.lib.util")`, `[RateLimitedSourceReader](<util/ratelimit/RateLimitedSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source.util.ratelimit")`, `[SingleThreadMultiplexSourceReaderBase](<../../../connector/base/source/reader/SingleThreadMultiplexSourceReaderBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader")`, `[SourceReaderBase](<../../../connector/base/source/reader/SourceReaderBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader")`, `[TestSourceReader](<../../../test/util/source/TestSourceReader.html> "class in org.apache.flink.test.util.source")`

* * *

[@Public](<../../../annotation/Public.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") public interface SourceReader<T,SplitT extends [SourceSplit](<SourceSplit.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")> extends [AutoCloseable](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/AutoCloseable.html> "class or interface in java.lang"), [CheckpointListener](<../../common/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.state")

The interface for a source reader which is responsible for reading the records from the source splits assigned by [`SplitEnumerator`](<SplitEnumerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source").

For most non-trivial source reader, it is recommended to use [`SourceReaderBase`](<../../../connector/base/source/reader/SourceReaderBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader") which provides an efficient hand-over protocol to avoid blocking I/O inside the task thread and supports various split-threading models.

Implementations can provide the following metrics:

  * [`OperatorIOMetricGroup.getNumRecordsInCounter()`](<../../../metrics/groups/OperatorIOMetricGroup.html#getNumRecordsInCounter\(\)>) (highly recommended)
  * [`OperatorIOMetricGroup.getNumBytesInCounter()`](<../../../metrics/groups/OperatorIOMetricGroup.html#getNumBytesInCounter\(\)>) (recommended)
  * [`SourceReaderMetricGroup.getNumRecordsInErrorsCounter()`](<../../../metrics/groups/SourceReaderMetricGroup.html#getNumRecordsInErrorsCounter\(\)>) (recommended)
  * [`SourceReaderMetricGroup.setPendingRecordsGauge(Gauge)`](<../../../metrics/groups/SourceReaderMetricGroup.html#setPendingRecordsGauge\(org.apache.flink.metrics.Gauge\)>)
  * [`SourceReaderMetricGroup.setPendingBytesGauge(Gauge)`](<../../../metrics/groups/SourceReaderMetricGroup.html#setPendingBytesGauge\(org.apache.flink.metrics.Gauge\)>)

  * ## Method Details

    * ### start

void start()

Start the reader.

    * ### pollNext

[InputStatus](<../../../core/io/InputStatus.html> "enum class in org.apache.flink.core.io") pollNext([ReaderOutput](<ReaderOutput.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source")<[T](<SourceReader.html> "type parameter in SourceReader")> output) throws [Exception](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Exception.html> "class or interface in java.lang")

Poll the next available record into the [`ReaderOutput`](<ReaderOutput.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source").

The implementation must make sure this method is non-blocking.

Although the implementation can emit multiple records into the given ReaderOutput, it is recommended not doing so. Instead, emit one record into the ReaderOutput and return a [`InputStatus.MORE_AVAILABLE`](<../../../core/io/InputStatus.html#MORE_AVAILABLE>) to let the caller thread know there are more records available.

Returns:
    The InputStatus of the SourceReader after the method invocation.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Exception.html> "class or interface in java.lang")`
    * ### snapshotState

[List](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/List.html> "class or interface in java.util")<[SplitT](<SourceReader.html> "type parameter in SourceReader")> snapshotState(long checkpointId)

Checkpoint on the state of the source.

Returns:
    the state of the source.
    * ### isAvailable

[CompletableFuture](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/concurrent/CompletableFuture.html> "class or interface in java.util.concurrent")<[Void](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Void.html> "class or interface in java.lang")> isAvailable()

Returns a future that signals that data is available from the reader.

Once the future completes, the runtime will keep calling the `pollNext(ReaderOutput)` method until that method returns a status other than [`InputStatus.MORE_AVAILABLE`](<../../../core/io/InputStatus.html#MORE_AVAILABLE>). After that, the runtime will again call this method to obtain the next future. Once that completes, it will again call `pollNext(ReaderOutput)` and so on.

The contract is the following: If the reader has data available, then all futures previously returned by this method must eventually complete. Otherwise the source might stall indefinitely.

It is not a problem to have occasional "false positives", meaning to complete a future even if no data is available. However, one should not use an "always complete" future in cases no data is available, because that will result in busy waiting loops calling ` pollNext(...)` even though no data is available.

Returns:
    a future that will be completed once there is a record available to poll.
    * ### addSplits

void addSplits([List](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/List.html> "class or interface in java.util")<[SplitT](<SourceReader.html> "type parameter in SourceReader")> splits)

Adds a list of splits for this reader to read. This method is called when the enumerator assigns a split via [`SplitEnumeratorContext.assignSplit(SourceSplit, int)`](<SplitEnumeratorContext.html#assignSplit\(SplitT,int\)>) or [`SplitEnumeratorContext.assignSplits(SplitsAssignment)`](<SplitEnumeratorContext.html#assignSplits\(org.apache.flink.api.connector.source.SplitsAssignment\)>).

Parameters:
    `splits` \- The splits assigned by the split enumerator.
    * ### notifyNoMoreSplits

void notifyNoMoreSplits()

This method is called when the reader is notified that it will not receive any further splits.

It is triggered when the enumerator calls [`SplitEnumeratorContext.signalNoMoreSplits(int)`](<SplitEnumeratorContext.html#signalNoMoreSplits\(int\)>) with the reader's parallel subtask.

    * ### handleSourceEvents

default void handleSourceEvents([SourceEvent](<SourceEvent.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source") sourceEvent)

Handle a custom source event sent by the [`SplitEnumerator`](<SplitEnumerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source"). This method is called when the enumerator sends an event via [`SplitEnumeratorContext.sendEventToSourceReader(int, SourceEvent)`](<SplitEnumeratorContext.html#sendEventToSourceReader\(int,org.apache.flink.api.connector.source.SourceEvent\)>).

This method has a default implementation that does nothing, because most sources do not require any custom events.

Parameters:
    `sourceEvent` \- the event sent by the [`SplitEnumerator`](<SplitEnumerator.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.connector.source").
    * ### notifyCheckpointComplete

default void notifyCheckpointComplete(long checkpointId) throws [Exception](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Exception.html> "class or interface in java.lang")

We have an empty default implementation here because most source readers do not have to implement the method.

Specified by:
    `[notifyCheckpointComplete](<../../common/state/CheckpointListener.html#notifyCheckpointComplete\(long\)>)` in interface `[CheckpointListener](<../../common/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.state")`
Parameters:
    `checkpointId` \- The ID of the checkpoint that has been completed.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Exception.html> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- This method can propagate exceptions, which leads to a failure/recovery for the task. Note that this will NOT lead to the checkpoint being revoked.
See Also:

      * [`CheckpointListener.notifyCheckpointComplete(long)`](<../../common/state/CheckpointListener.html#notifyCheckpointComplete\(long\)>)
    * ### pauseOrResumeSplits

[@PublicEvolving](<../../../annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation interface in org.apache.flink.annotation") default void pauseOrResumeSplits([Collection](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Collection.html> "class or interface in java.util")<[String](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html> "class or interface in java.lang")> splitsToPause, [Collection](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Collection.html> "class or interface in java.util")<[String](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html> "class or interface in java.lang")> splitsToResume)

Pauses or resumes reading of individual source splits.

Note that no other methods can be called in parallel, so updating subscriptions can be done atomically. This method is simply providing connectors with more expressive APIs the opportunity to update all subscriptions at once.

This is currently used to align the watermarks of splits, if watermark alignment is used and the source reads from more than one split.

The default implementation throws an [`UnsupportedOperationException`](<https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/UnsupportedOperationException.html> "class or interface in java.lang") where the default implementation will be removed in future releases. To be compatible with future releases, it is recommended to implement this method and override the default implementation.

Parameters:
    `splitsToPause` \- the splits to pause
    `splitsToResume` \- the splits to resume
